metric_value: Intel's 3rd Annual NDA Industry Analyst Business & Technology Summit 'Labs, Fab and Gab' — Feb 24-25, 2009; Day 1 at RA3 (2501 NW 229th Ave, Hillsboro OR 97124), Day 2 at Jones Farm Conference Center (2111 NE 25th Ave).
Peter Kastner attending as Scott-Page Associates. Personalized schedule noted a few items in blue = 'meetings not on the main event agenda (i.e. invite only).'
Day 1 9:00-10:00 AM: Intel Green / Eco-Tech Huddle with Lorie Wigle (RA3-MPR3B); 11:00 AM-12:00 PM: Emerging Market Roundtable (RA3-TR1).
Day 1 12:00-12:50 PM: Sign in / Lunch / D1D Fab Window Tour.
Day 1 1:00-1:45 PM: Sean Maloney (Executive VP & Chief Sales & Marketing Officer) keynote.
Day 1 1:45-2:20 PM: William Holt (Senior VP; GM of Technology and Manufacturing Group).
Day 1 2:35-3:05 PM: Andy Bryant (Executive VP & Chief Administrative Officer).
Day 1 3:05-3:30 PM: Kirk Skaugen (VP; GM of Server Products Group).
Day 1 3:30-3:55 PM: Rob Crooke (VP; GM of Business Client Group).
Day 1 3:55-4:15 PM: Chuck Mulloy (Sr. Manager, Corporate & Legal Affairs).
Day 1 5:30-8:00 PM: cocktail reception with Intel executives at McMenamins Cornelius Pass Roadhouse (4045 NW Cornelius Pass Rd, Hillsboro OR; 503-640-6174).

Day 2 lab tours: (1) Microprocessor Validation Lab — CPU post-silicon validation, platforms, debug tools; (2) vPro Technology Business Client & Enterprise Platform Integration Lab; (3) DataCenter Tour.

Day 2 DataCenter Tour description: Intel's newest lights-out data center at Jones Farm Campus — built as part of Intel's datacenter consolidation, two 6,000-square-foot modules each operating at over 500 watts per square foot.
Day 2 11:00-11:50 AM: Justin Rattner (Intel Senior Fellow; VP; Director of Corporate Technology Group and Intel CTO).
Day 2 12:00-1:15 PM: Lunch with Peter Cleveland (VP, Legal and Corporate Affairs / Director, Global Public Policy).

Day 2 Breakout #1: Notebook Strategies & Plans — Karen Regis and Uday Marty (Mobile Products Group Marketing Managers) give 2009 strategies/plans overview, 2010 sneak peek, next-gen platform roadmap update.
Day 2 Breakout #2: Larrabee Programmability — overview of Larrabee architecture and the new LRBni (Larrabee New Instructions) vector instruction-set.
notes: Larrabee later discontinued as GPU (2010), repurposed to Xeon Phi.

metric_value: Day 2 Breakout #3: Mobile Internet Platforms — Erik Reid (Dir of Mobile Products Group Marketing) and Gary Willihnganz (Dir of Ultra Mobile Group Marketing) roundtable on Intel's MID, netbooks, and nettop strategy/product plans.
Day 2 4:15-5:00 PM: Intel Experts Panel concluding the summit.
